ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for spaghetti with fresh tomato sauce and burrata; closest_parent 'pasta food product' (FOODON_00002473) used as the nearest defensible ancestor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC (Pasta, cooked) is the closest defensible parent; no specific code exists for spaghetti pomodoro with burrata as a composite dish.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 229961009 'Pasta dish' is the closest defensible parent concept for this composite Italian pasta dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of spaghetti with fresh tomato sauce, basil, and burrata is approximately 350 g, comprising cooked pasta, tomato sauce, a portion of burrata cheese, fresh basil leaves, and a drizzle of extra virgin olive oil.

nutrient_source.title: Spaghetti with tomato sauce and cheese — composite estimate from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries: Spaghetti cooked (NDB 20124), Tomato sauce (NDB 11549), Mozzarella/Burrata-type fresh cheese (NDB 01009), Olive oil (NDB 04053), Fresh basil (NDB 02044)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA or HPB entry exists for spaghetti pomodoro fresco with burrata as a composite restaurant dish. Values are estimated from a weighted blend of USDA SR Legacy entries for cooked spaghetti (~55% by weight), fresh tomato sauce (~25%), fresh mozzarella/burrata-type cheese (~15%), olive oil (~3%), and fresh basil (~2%). Burrata is nutritionally similar to fresh mozzarella (USDA NDB 01009) and is treated as such. Per-100g values reflect the blended dish as plated.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; spaghetti boiled (white, average of multiple studies, GI ~46)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal (peer-reviewed)
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 46 is taken from the University of Sydney GI Database for boiled white spaghetti (average of multiple published studies, reference food = glucose). This is applied to the composite dish as the pasta component dominates the glycemic response. The fresh tomato sauce, burrata, basil, and olive oil contribute negligible additional glycemic load. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (46 × 69.3) / 100 ≈ 32.0, where carbohydrate per 350 g serving = 19.8 × 3.5 = 69.3 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~185 mg/100 g; ~648 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: A single restaurant serving contributes approximately 648 mg sodium (~28% of the 2,300 mg daily limit recommended by the American Heart Association), so individuals with hypertension should monitor portion size and request reduced-salt preparation where possible.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~32 per serving) driven by refined spaghetti carbohydrates
type2_diabetes.guidance: Although spaghetti has a relatively low GI (~46), the large carbohydrate load per restaurant serving (~69 g) produces a moderate glycemic load of approximately 32, which may cause a meaningful postprandial glucose rise; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consider a smaller portion or substitute wholegrain pasta to reduce the glycemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Predominantly unsaturated fat from extra virgin olive oil; moderate saturated fat from burrata
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The dish provides a favourable fat profile overall due to the extra virgin olive oil, but the burrata contributes saturated fat (~2.1 g/100 g; ~7.4 g per serving); individuals with cardiovascular disease should be mindful of total saturated fat intake and consider a smaller burrata portion.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Spaghetti with Fresh Tomato Sauce, Basil, and Burrata is a classic Italian pasta dish consisting of al dente spaghetti tossed in a vibrant fresh tomato sauce (pomodoro fresco), garnished with torn fresh basil leaves, a generous portion of Burrata DOP (a soft, creamy Italian cheese made from mozzarella and cream), and finished with a drizzle of organic extra virgin olive oil. The dish is vegetarian and gluten-containing. The texture contrasts firm pasta strands with the silky, milky interior of the burrata, while the fresh tomato sauce provides bright acidity balanced by the richness of the cheese and the grassy, peppery notes of the olive oil. In Singapore, this dish is served at Italian restaurants such as Ricciotti at The Riverwalk and is positioned as a premium vegetarian pasta option. Nutritionally, a standard restaurant serving (~350 g) provides approximately 518 kcal, 20 g protein, 18 g fat, and 69 g carbohydrate. The glycemic index of the pasta component is approximately 46 (low GI), though the glycemic load per serving is moderate (~32) due to the large carbohydrate portion. The dish is a moderate source of calcium from the burrata, provides beneficial monounsaturated fats from olive oil, and contains lycopene from the tomato sauce. Clinically, the sodium content (~648 mg per serving) warrants attention for individuals managing hypertension, and the carbohydrate load requires portion control for those with Type 2 Diabetes.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for this exact composite restaurant dish. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted blend of USDA SR Legacy entries for the individual components (cooked spaghetti, fresh tomato sauce, fresh mozzarella as a burrata proxy, olive oil, fresh basil). GI is taken from the University of Sydney GI Database for boiled white spaghetti as the dominant glycemic component. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component sources are high-quality and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for individual components; no single database entry exists for this exact dish. 2. Burrata DOP is treated as nutritionally equivalent to fresh mozzarella (USDA NDB 01009) as no separate USDA entry for burrata exists; burrata has a slightly higher fat and cream content than standard fresh mozzarella, so fat values may be marginally underestimated. 3. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy component source entries for this dish composition. 4. trans_fat_g is 0.0; confirmed negligible/trace in all component sources (USDA SR Legacy). 5. GI of 46 is from the University of Sydney GI Database for boiled white spaghetti (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care); applied as analogous estimate to the composite dish. 6. Serving size of 350 g is an estimate for a standard Italian restaurant pasta main course; actual restaurant portion at Ricciotti may vary. 7. The (V) suffix in the original food name denotes vegetarian; is_vegetarian is set to true. 8. Halal status is not confirmed from the input data; is_halal is set to false pending verification. 9. This is an Italian cuisine dish; multilingual naming rules for Singaporean dishes do not apply. No mandatory Chinese, Malay, Tamil, or Hindi names are required under Project Veritas v2 multilingual rules for Italian cuisine. 10. Glycemic load per serving = (46 × 69.3) / 100 ≈ 32.0, where 69.3 g carbohydrate = 19.8 g/100g × 350 g serving.

improvement_actions_taken: Constructed composite nutrient estimate from USDA SR Legacy component entries as no single source entry exists for this dish.
Applied GI of 46 from University of Sydney GI Database for boiled white spaghetti as analogous estimate and documented basis.
Revised documentation for `vitamin_d_mcg = -1` in `notes` to explicitly state it is 'not reported' in the source, addressing G3 numeric hygiene failure.
Confirmed trans_fat_g = 0.0 as trace/negligible per USDA SR Legacy; no zero-macro justification required as no core macro is zero.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases are perfectly consistent — all strings in aliases_by_language appear verbatim in aliases.
Confirmed Italian cuisine does not trigger Singaporean multilingual naming requirements; G6 satisfied.
Set ontology match_type values to only allowed strings: exact, closest_parent, or none.
Documented all -1 sentinel values in notes field.
